name: PTQ
oneliner: Quantize an already-trained model without further training — fast (minutes to hours), practical, dominates LLM quantization.
explanation: Takes a fully trained model and converts to lower precision in one pass. No training infrastructure needed. Methods: GPTQ, AWQ, RTN, EXL2. Fast even for 70B models. Downside: model never adapted to quantization noise, so quality degrades more than QAT, especially below 4-bit. At int8: essentially lossless. At int4: measurable but acceptable. At int2: usually unacceptable without QAT.
fundamentals: Workflow: 1) Load fp16/bf16 model. 2) Optional calibration pass. 3) Compute scale/zero-point per layer/channel/group. 4) Quantize: W_q = round(W/scale) + zp. 5) Save. At inference: dequantize and compute.

resources.label: White Paper on Neural Network Quantization
